about: Per-zone plantar load + PRESSURE (kPa) + anatomical mm positions + kinetic-chain injury links, per movement. Flags OVER/UNDER-used regions and the injuries they predict. Confidence per value: 'published' (a value/range in a cited paper), 'published_order' (rank from a paper; magnitudes fitted), 'estimated' (no direct source yet). v0.3 adds real per-zone peak pressures (kPa) for walking/standing/running/basketball and clinical thresholds. Bottom-of-foot focus.

foot_reference.note: positions are for a 255 mm foot; scale by your length (zone_load.py --foot-length-mm).

units.force: peak vertical GRF in body weights (x BW)

sensor_map.note: 8-FSR rig senses these; met2 & met4 are INTERPOLATED.

clinical_thresholds.IMPORTANT: These are DIABETIC/NEUROPATHIC-tissue ulcer-risk thresholds (~67% of studies use 200 kPa in-shoe). HEALTHY athletic tissue tolerates far higher transiently — running forefoot ~300-560 kPa, basketball-landing hallux ~800 kPa are normal. Use 200 kPa as a SUSTAINED-load caution (at-risk feet), NOT an athletic limit.

walk_norm.cite: Plantar pressure assessment in healthy adults, barefoot walking (MatScan/TekScan)
walk_norm.finding: peak kPa: hallux 280, heel 264, met1 248, met2 246, met3 225

stand_norm.cite: Cavanagh et al., pressure distribution under symptom-free feet, barefoot standing
stand_norm.finding: load heel 60% / midfoot 8% / forefoot 28%; peak heel 139 kPa vs forefoot 53 kPa (heel 2.6x)

run_press.cite: Foot orthoses & forefoot peak plantar pressure in running, 2016

run_slope.cite: Foot-strike pattern & plantar pressure, uphill/downhill running, Front. Sports, 2025
run_slope.finding: rearfoot-strike mid/hind ~190 kPa early; heel +32% downhill, -24% uphill

court_press.cite: Regional foot pressure in running/cutting/jumping/landing; basketball vs soccer plantar patterns (LER)
court_press.finding: basketball peaks central-forefoot 50% / hallux 25%; landing hallux 79.4 N/cm^2 (=794 kPa), forefoot 40.3 N/cm^2 (=403 kPa)

ulcer_thr.cite: Plantar pressure thresholds to prevent diabetic foot ulcers: systematic review, 2024
ulcer_thr.finding: 200 kPa in-shoe mean peak pressure = most-agreed target; 450 & 750 kPa barefoot

gym_grf.cite: Biomechanics of landing in gymnasts: scoping review, Front. Sports, 2025

skate_grf.cite: Landing GRF in figure skaters; ACSM 'Science of Figure Skating'

dance_press.finding: 1st-MTP pain -> less hallux, more met-head load

hsi_sprint.cite: Hamstring strain in sprinting (PMC6188997); soccer HSI video review, 2026
hsi_sprint.finding: late-swing eccentric just before foot-strike; overstride worsens; #1 soccer injury

acl_grf.finding: high vGRF + loading rate predicts ACL; strain peaks with vGRF

pose_grf.cite: vGRF in single-leg landing from 2D video + pose AI, 2024

pf_press.cite: Biomechanical parameters in plantar fasciitis by gait analysis (PMC5773441); PF athletes plantar-pressure features
pf_press.finding: PF = altered medial/lateral-heel peak pressure (deficit OR excess) + deformed pressure vector; forefoot/met overstrain feeds the fascia; first-step morning pain

itw_press.cite: Idiopathic toe walking: plantar pressure & toe function (PubMed 31946390); ITW update, JAAOS, 2022
itw_press.finding: ITW ~61.7% forefoot / 15.3% midfoot / 22.8% hindfoot contact vs normal ~39% forefoot / 30% rearfoot; increased forefoot & decreased hindfoot callosity

asd_gait.cite: Gait patterns in ASD (systematic review); 8-week exercise program in ASD boys, 2023
asd_gait.finding: forefoot-loading tendency + more L/R asymmetry & step variability; intervention reduced 1st-peak vGRF, loading rate, and MEDIAL-HEEL peak pressure

equinus_press.cite: Ankle-joint flexibility & barefoot plantar pressures, healthy older adults (PMC9338503)
equinus_press.finding: ankle equinus forefoot peak 677.8 kPa (589.9-810.4) vs 565.6 (447.3-651.2) without equinus; <10 deg dorsiflexion -> premature heel rise -> forefoot overload -> metatarsalgia / met stress fx / plantar plate / sesamoiditis / PF

pti_norm.cite: Plantar pressure-time integral (air-insole & walking-duration study), Sci Reports, 2024
pti_norm.finding: PTI = area under the peak-pressure-time curve during stance; met2 ~38.4 kPa*s, met1 ~44-58 kPa*s. Running = HIGHER peak but LOWER PTI (shorter contact). PTI is a BETTER injury/tissue-breakdown predictor than peak. 4 forefoot regions (hallux, M1, M2-3, M4-5) most ulcer-susceptible

heel_lift.cite: In-shoe heel lifts & plantar pressure (PubMed 24440428); optimized heel-lift design (J Biomech 2016)
heel_lift.finding: raising the heel ATTENUATES heel peak pressure but INCREASES forefoot pressure + PTI; a lift alone trades heel pain for forefoot overload. PORON ~15 Shore reduces the downside; optimized lifts that preserve midfoot function improve distribution

cut_press.cite: In-shoe tri-axial stress during max-effort cutting (J Biomech 2014); basketball maneuver plantar loads (PMC5941807)
cut_press.finding: cutting PEAK pressure at 2nd met head; max SHEAR at 1st met head (lateral-shuffle medial shear 312.2 kPa; 45-deg sidestep propulsion shear 463.0 kPa). Forefoot/MTPj = primary contact -> 1st & 2nd met injury-susceptible

hv_press.cite: Plantar-pressure transfer medial->central forefoot in hallux valgus, BMC Musculoskelet Disord, 2019
hv_press.finding: hallux pressure DECREASED (affected 197 vs contralateral 221 kPa); load transfers medial (hallux/met1) -> central forefoot

seat_load.cite: Loading the feet in seating (clinical seating); differentiating sitting/standing/walking by regional plantar pressure
seat_load.finding: ~19% of body weight passes through the feet in normal sitting; forefoot-vs-heel share shifts with seat/footplate angle (25 deg tilt -> forefoot)

tap_freq.cite: Sensor-based quantification of repetitive lower-limb movements (MDS-UPDRS-III), 2024; insole foot-tap tests
tap_freq.finding: toe-tap median frequency ~2.8 Hz (~16 deg), heel-down; ball-of-foot force cycling — the biomechanics of leg-bounce / fidget

cpts.cite: Cumulative plantar tissue stress (PTI x steps/day) & diabetic-ulcer healing
cpts.finding: CPTS = pressure-time-integral x daily step count (MPa.s/day). Healed ulcers accumulated 49% LESS daily stress (140 vs 275 MPa.s/day). The all-day dose predicts tissue outcome better than pressure or activity alone.

cpts_mtp.cite: PTI & cumulative plantar stress vs first-MTP joint pain (community study)
cpts_mtp.finding: Higher hallux/1st-MTP pressure-time integral & cumulative stress associate with first-MTP joint pain -- CPTS relates to musculoskeletal pain, not just ulcers.

press_grad.cite: Pressure gradient & subsurface shear stress on the neuropathic forefoot
press_grad.finding: A steep spatial pressure GRADIENT (sharp change around the peak) concentrates internal/subsurface shear near nerves & soft tissue -- more discriminating for breakdown risk than peak pressure alone; forefoot shear sits closest to the surface where skin breaks down.

morton.cite: Morton neuroma -- interdigital nerve compressive neuropathy (StatPearls)
morton.finding: Compression of the common digital nerve, usually 3rd (met3-met4) then 2nd (met2-met3) interspace -- those spaces are narrower -> higher pressure. Forefoot loading + toe hyperextension (relevE, heels, toe-walking) tethers the nerve under the transverse intermetatarsal ligament.

baxter.cite: Baxter's nerve (inferior calcaneal n., 1st branch lateral plantar n.) entrapment
baxter.finding: Compressive medial heel-pain neuropathy, up to ~20% of chronic heel pain; entrapped at the abductor hallucis fascial edge / thickened plantar fascia / heel spur. Unlike plantar fasciitis (worst first steps in the morning), Baxter pain WORSENS WITH WEIGHT-BEARING THROUGH THE DAY -- an all-day dose signature.

tarsal_tunnel.cite: Tarsal tunnel syndrome -- posterior tibial nerve entrapment (StatPearls)
tarsal_tunnel.finding: Compression/traction of the tibial nerve at the medial ankle; OVERPRONATION places the nerve under increased tension. Medial-arch support/orthoses are part of conservative care.

pf_windlass.cite: Plantar fasciitis & the windlass mechanism (biomechanical link)
pf_windlass.finding: Toe/hallux extension + heel-lift TENSIONS the plantar aponeurosis into a rigid lever (windlass). Fascia is loaded in TENSION, not just compression; the medial calcaneal insertion is where it fails.

equinus_pf.cite: Equinus / tight gastroc-soleus and plantar fascia load
equinus_pf.finding: >80% of plantar fasciitis patients have equinus; equinus -> 23x plantar-fasciitis risk. A tight calf (gastroc-soleus-PF continuum) forces early forefoot loading, raises met-head pressure, AND increases fascial tension via the windlass.

metrics.peak_pressure_kPa: the single highest pressure a region sees (magnitude).
metrics.pressure_time_integral_kPa_s: area under the region's pressure-time curve during stance = cumulative dose. **Better predictor of overuse/tissue-breakdown injury than peak** (pti_norm). interpret.py's per-zone 'impulse' IS the calibrated PTI.
metrics.peak_vs_pti: Running raises PEAK but lowers PTI (short contact); walking/standing accumulate PTI over time. A high-peak brief event and a low-peak sustained one injure differently — track both.

cumulative_plantar_tissue_stress_MPa_s_d.formula: CPTS_zone = (PTI_per_cycle in MPa.s) x (loading cycles per day)
cumulative_plantar_tissue_stress_MPa_s_d.description: The ALL-DAY dose: peak pressure is one step; PTI is one step's dose; CPTS is a whole day's dose. This is the metric that connects the pressure we log throughout the day to nerve/fascia overload. Forefoot cycles/day = gait steps + the at-rest leg-bounce/toe-tap dose (bounce.py); heel cycles ~= gait steps.
cumulative_plantar_tissue_stress_MPa_s_d.IMPORTANT: 140 vs 275 MPa.s/day are DIABETIC-ULCER healing/non-healing figures -- an order-of-magnitude anchor, NOT a healthy-tissue limit. Use CPTS to compare zones and track your OWN trend over time, not as a diagnostic cutoff.

pressure_gradient_kPa_per_zone.description: Spatial change in pressure between adjacent zones. A STEEP gradient (a sharp jump next to a hot zone) concentrates subsurface shear near nerves/soft tissue and discriminates breakdown risk better than peak alone. Forefoot gradients matter most (shear sits nearest the surface there).

heel_lift.effect: attenuates HEEL peak pressure, but raises FOREFOOT pressure + PTI
heel_lift.design_rule: for medial heel pain WITH equinus/toe-walking (already forefoot-loaded), a heel lift alone just moves the problem forward -> pair heel offload WITH forefoot cushioning (met pad / soft forefoot) so you don't trade heel pain for metatarsalgia. Softer lift (PORON ~15 Shore) + preserved midfoot beats a hard flat lift.

footwear_effects.note: footwear reshapes the whole map; barefoot norms are the reference, in-shoe is what you live in (200 kPa in-shoe threshold applies there).

condition_notes_footwear: For the equinus/toe-walker: a raised heel reduces the dorsiflexion demand (helps the calf) AND offloads the painful heel — but it pushes load onto an already-overloaded forefoot. The winning insole does BOTH: heel lift/cushion + forefoot offload (met pad) + preserved arch; and pairs it with LOADING the calf/plantar (hypermobile), not just stretching.
condition_notes: CONDITION profiles describe a person's adaptive/altered BASELINE (not a sport). Use zone_load.py --condition so an ADAPTIVE pattern (e.g. habitual toe-walking) is NOT false-flagged as pathology — low heel loading is that person's norm. The engine then tracks the condition's REAL risks (forefoot overload trend, calf/dorsiflexion) and deviation from the condition baseline. Management is person-specific: tight non-hypermobile calf -> mobilize/eccentric; HYPERMOBILE -> LOAD/strengthen, do not over-stretch a lax system.

chain_rules._note: foot-loading SIGNATURES -> kinetic-chain injuries (foot-local AND proximal).

overstride_forefoot_late.signal: foot lands ahead of body in sprint

peak_force_BW.note: per foot; bodyweight split across two feet

standing.injury: heel bears ~60% in stance (139 kPa, 2.6x forefoot)

walking.injury: hallux/heel/central-met are the gait peak-pressure regions
walking.chain_injury: the clinical baseline; medial collapse -> PF / MTSS

running_rearfoot.chain_injury: over-stride -> hamstring; medial collapse -> shin splints / PF

peak_pressure_kPa.confidence: forefoot published; per-zone estimated from it
hallux & forefoot published (N/cm^2 x10); others estimated

basketball_landing.injury: hallux (turf toe / sesamoid) + central-met; wooden court raises hallux vs asphalt

gymnastics_landing.injury: central-met & heel stress from very high BW impacts

figure_skating_landing.movement: single-leg jump landing, back outside edge

ballet_releve.injury: 1st-MTP overload -> hallux valgus/sesamoiditis; hallux UNDER-load shifts to met2 (dancer's fracture)

ballet_pointe.injury: base of 2nd-met stress fx ('dancer's fracture'); hallux/1st-ray extreme
ballet_pointe.chain_injury: COG onto met shafts -> ankle / post-tib

soccer_sprint.chain_injury: **HAMSTRING strain** — late-swing eccentric just before foot-strike (#1 soccer injury); over-stride lengthens the eccentric load

peak_pressure_kPa.note: PEAK pressure at met2; max SHEAR at 1st-met head 312-463 kPa (lateral-shuffle braking 312, 45-deg-sidestep propulsion 463)

jump_landing.chain_injury: **ACL** — high vGRF + loading rate + L/R asymmetry; land soft & symmetric

plantar_fasciitis.movement: walking with plantar heel pain (medial calcaneal / PF)

peak_pressure_kPa.confidence: medial-heel PP altered (deficit OR excess); direction varies by person

plantar_fasciitis.dist_confidence: estimated (altered heel + compensatory forefoot per PF literature)
plantar_fasciitis.injury: plantar fasciitis / medial calcaneal heel pain — first-step morning pain; deformed heel pressure vector; forefoot/met overstrain tugs the fascia (soft-tissue, NOT a stress fracture)
plantar_fasciitis.chain_injury: avoiding the heel -> forefoot overload + calf/Achilles tightness (equinus loop)
plantar_fasciitis.management_note: offload the medial heel + restore ankle dorsiflexion. Hypermobile -> LOAD/strengthen the plantar/calf, don't just stretch.

toe_walking.movement: habitual toe / forefoot walking (idiopathic or adaptive)
toe_walking.injury: forefoot overload -> metatarsalgia / met stress; toe-grip (flexor) overactivity; wider forefoot + forefoot callus
toe_walking.chain_injury: chronic plantarflexion -> gastroc/Achilles tightness (equinus). LOW heel loading is the ADAPTIVE NORM here, not a deficit
toe_walking.baseline_note: For a HABITUAL toe-walker this IS the baseline — do NOT flag 'heel under-used' as pathology. Track forefoot-overload TRENDS + calf/ankle dorsiflexion instead. Toe-walking may be pain-avoidant (heel pain), sensory/quieting, or a retained pattern.
toe_walking.management_note: reversing it isn't always the goal; if kept, protect the forefoot (met pad, cushion) + keep ankle dorsiflexion. Hypermobile -> LOAD, don't over-stretch.

equinus.movement: limited ankle dorsiflexion (tight gastroc/Achilles) during gait
equinus.dist_confidence: estimated (<10 deg dorsiflexion -> premature heel rise -> forefoot overload)
equinus.injury: metatarsalgia, met stress fx, plantar plate tear, sesamoiditis, plantar fasciitis (forefoot overload from restricted dorsiflexion)
equinus.chain_injury: the common thread behind toe-walking + heel-pain avoidance; drives PF + forefoot problems
equinus.management_note: restore dorsiflexion to drop forefoot pressure. Tight NON-hypermobile calf -> eccentric/mobilize; HYPERMOBILE -> LOAD/strengthen (a lax system needs control, not more stretch); severe/resistant -> gastroc recession is the surgical option.

seated_bounce.movement: seated leg-bounce / toe-tap (at-rest forefoot loading — the 'jiggle')

peak_force_BW.note: ~19% BW through the feet in sitting; forefoot-dominant when bouncing (heel-down toe-tap)

seated_bounce.injury: cumulative FOREFOOT dose (pressure-time integral) with little rest -> metatarsalgia / sesamoiditis / met stress in forefoot-loaders
seated_bounce.chain_injury: at ~2.8 Hz x hours = TENS OF THOUSANDS of forefoot cycles/day — often MORE than daily walking steps; invisible to step counters; stacks on gait
seated_bounce.baseline_note: A self-regulating / stim movement (ADHD / autism / anxiety). The goal is to DOSE it (soft forefoot pad while seated, vary the pattern), NOT to suppress it. analysis/bounce.py quantifies the hidden dose (cycles/day + forefoot PTI).
seated_bounce.management_note: protect the forefoot during long bouncing (soft met pad / cushion), give the ball of the foot rest windows; don't pathologize the movement itself.

structures.about: Maps the zone pressure/PTI/CPTS we measure onto the anatomical NERVE and FASCIA structures that specific loading patterns overload. Associations (which structure, which zones, which loading mode, the differentiating clue) are published; the zone weights & bands used by nerve_fascia.py are estimated from anatomy + these papers. SCREENING/education, NOT diagnosis -- these structures overlap and only a clinician (often with ultrasound/MRI or a diagnostic block) can confirm which is which.

plantar_fascia.driver: heel-lift + toe/hallux extension tensions it; equinus/tight gastroc forces early forefoot loading and raises met-head pressure -> more fascial tension. Toe-walking/forefoot-loading stacks the dose.
plantar_fascia.differentiator: medial calcaneal insertion; classic FIRST-STEP MORNING pain, worse after rest.
plantar_fascia.manage: offload medial heel AND cushion the forefoot; calf/soleus length work; hallux/windlass load management. Hypermobile: load, don't over-stretch.

baxter_nerve.aka: inferior calcaneal nerve = first branch of the lateral plantar nerve
baxter_nerve.mode: compression (entrapment under abductor hallucis / thickened fascia / spur)
baxter_nerve.driver: sustained medial-heel weight-bearing dose through the day; a thickened/inflamed plantar fascia squeezes it.
baxter_nerve.differentiator: pain slightly MORE MEDIAL than the fascia insertion; unlike fasciitis it WORSENS THROUGH THE DAY with weight-bearing (an all-day-dose signature), often burning/constant; Tinel over the medial calcaneus radiates to the lateral heel / 4th-5th toes.
baxter_nerve.manage: the same medial-heel offload helps, but if heel pain is worse-with-the-day (not worse-in-the-morning) and resists fasciitis care, this nerve is the suspect -> clinician for US-guided block / MRI.

morton_neuroma.aka: interdigital / intermetatarsal neuroma (common digital plantar nerve)
morton_neuroma.mode: compression between met heads + toe-hyperextension tethering under the transverse intermetatarsal ligament
morton_neuroma.driver: forefoot loading + toe extension. The at-rest forefoot BOUNCE/toe-tap dose (bounce.py) stacks onto gait here -- narrow 2nd/3rd interspaces already run higher pressure.
morton_neuroma.differentiator: BURNING / numbness / tingling into adjacent toes, 'pebble in the shoe', Mulder's click; relieved by removing the shoe & splaying the toes.
morton_neuroma.manage: widen the toe box, a metatarsal pad PROXIMAL to the met heads to splay them, cut forefoot dose / add ball-of-foot rest windows, avoid sustained toe-hyperextension.

tibial_nerve_tarsal_tunnel.aka: posterior tibial nerve (tarsal tunnel, medial ankle)
tibial_nerve_tarsal_tunnel.driver: an overpronation / medial-loading signature (heel_med >> heel_lat, heavy medial midfoot) puts the nerve under traction at the medial ankle.
tibial_nerve_tarsal_tunnel.differentiator: medial-ANKLE Tinel; burning/tingling radiating into the sole & toes; worse with activity/standing.
tibial_nerve_tarsal_tunnel.manage: medial-arch support / motion control to reduce pronation traction; if radiating medial-ankle symptoms -> clinician.
